svg.text: Byteword / SAT Framing Spec (not plain English tokenization)
Goal: encode logic-bearing structure first, natural language second, with deterministic SAT-oriented symbols.
1) Token classes (vocab target: 8192)
[SAT_OP] AND OR NOT XOR IMPLIES IFF EXISTS FORALL
[SAT_META] ASSERT PREMISE GOAL PROOF_STEP CONTRADICTION FRONTIER
[DOMAIN] CODE_, MATH_, BIO_, LAW_, TEMP_ prefixes
[BYTE] b00 ... b255 fallback atoms
[CTRL] <bos> <eos> <pad> <sep> <domain:...>
Do not train on raw bitstream tokens directly (8x sequence inflation).
Bytewords preserve compression + symbolic semantics for SAT trajectories.
Expected byte fallback rate after SAT canonicalization: < 12%
2) Canonicalization pipeline
a) Normalize unicode, punctuation, and equation spacing
b) Convert to SAT DSL skeleton (operators + scoped symbols)
c) Attach trajectory markers: premise -> transform -> verdict
d) Run SAT gate labels: VERIFIED / FRONTIER / CONTRADICTION / TIMEOUT
e) Tokenize to bytewords, then fallback bXX where unresolved
f) Emit packed records with domain tags and reward priors
Example record key: {domain, sat_form, trajectory, verdict, reward}
Invariant: identical text must canonicalize to identical SAT form hash.
3) Dataset mixture and sampling policy
Base mix: 40% VERIFIED trajectories, 25% FRONTIER, 20% near-contradiction repairs, 15% hard negatives
Expert mixes: Proof-heavy / Code-heavy / Math-heavy / Domain blend (all keep SAT verdict labels)
Sampler: temperature by contradiction rarity + per-domain floor to avoid expert collapse.
Batch schema frozen across base, REAP calibration, expert FT, and router stages.
Faith-test objective implemented: quality via SAT framing, not generic internet token mass.
4) Validation checks before training
[ ] Canonicalization collision rate < 0.05%
[ ] Byte fallback distribution stable across train/val/test
[ ] SAT verdict labels reproducible (same input, same label) on two independent runs
[ ] Tokenizer checksum committed with checkpoint metadata
File: docs/weekend_pipeline_04_byteword_spec.svg
